macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Hits New Record High Amid US-Iran Talks Optimism
items.summary: US stocks closed higher with S&P 500 up 1.2% to 6,967, Nasdaq advancing 2%, and Dow gaining 318 points, driven by hopes of US-Iran talks to reopen Strait of Hormuz. Communication services and consumer discretionary led gains while energy lagged on falling oil prices.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge and Remain Volatile Due to Middle East Conflict
items.summary: Oil prices have spiked amid ongoing Middle East tensions, pushing CPI inflation to 3.3% in March from 2.4% in February, while core inflation held at 2.6%. SF Fed warns of clouded economic outlook with higher input costs for fertilizers, aluminum, and plastics.

items.title: Mixed Bank Earnings: JPMorgan and Wells Fargo Drop, BlackRock and Citi Rise
items.summary: JPMorgan fell 0.7% after cutting net interest income guidance, Wells Fargo tumbled 5.7% on weak results, while BlackRock gained 3% and Citigroup 2.6% on strong earnings. J&J rebounded 0.9% after raising 2026 revenue outlook.

items.title: American Airlines Jumps 8% on Potential Merger Reports
items.summary: American Airlines shares surged 8% following reports that United Airlines CEO proposed a merger. This came amid broader market gains buoyed by geopolitical optimism.

items.title: Argentina Inflation Ticks Up to 2.9% Monthly Amid Middle East War Fears
items.summary: Argentina's monthly inflation rose to 2.9% in early 2026, with full-year forecasts revised higher due to removed price controls, utility hikes, and expected energy/food price impacts from Middle East conflict. President Milei's reforms show partial success but face stalling stabilization.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds at $74,000 with 5% Rally as Market Cap Hits $2.52 Trillion
items.summary: Bitcoin rallied 5.15% to $74,438 and Ethereum jumped 8.80% toward $2,400, expanding the total crypto market cap by 4.53% to $2.52 trillion amid a short squeeze liquidating $549 million, mostly shorts.

items.title: $321 Million in Crypto Contracts Liquidated Across Network
items.summary: Total cryptocurrency futures liquidations reached $321 million in the past 24 hours, with $170 million in longs and $151 million in shorts; BTC liquidations at $107 million and ETH at $85 million.

items.title: XRP Surges 4.4% to $1.40 on Ripple's Partnership with Kyobo Life Insurance
items.summary: Ripple's XRP led gains among major cryptos with a 4.4% jump after announcing a partnership with South Korea’s Kyobo Life Insurance to tokenize government bond settlement, flipping retail sentiment to bullish.

items.title: RaveDAO, Polkadot, and Ethena Lead Altcoin Gains as Bitcoin Stabilizes
items.summary: Bitcoin holds steady at $74,000 amid Trump's 'good deal' comment, while RaveDAO, Polkadot, and Ethena extend gains over the last 24 hours; overall market up 0.9% to $2.6 trillion.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation Executive Josh Stark Announces Departure
items.summary: Josh Stark, executive at the Ethereum Foundation, has announced his departure, marking a notable change in Ethereum's leadership.

items.title: Uniswap Launches Official Developer Platform with API Dashboards and AI Toolkits
items.summary: Uniswap has released the official version of its developer platform, introducing API dashboards and AI toolkits to enhance development.

items.title: Rhea Finance Hit by Attack, $7.6 Million Stolen via Fake Token Contracts
items.summary: Security agency reports Rhea Finance suffered an attack involving fake token contracts, resulting in approximately $7.6 million stolen.

items.title: Spektr Raises $20 Million in Series A Funding for Financial Compliance
items.summary: Financial compliance startup Spektr, focused on crypto, secured $20 million in Series A funding led by NEA.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Opus 4.7, Best Public AI Coding Model
items.summary: Claude Opus 4.7 achieves 64.3% on SWE-Bench Pro, surpassing previous version's 53.4% and competitors like GPT 5.4 (57.7%) and Gemini 3.1 Pro (54.2%). Anthropic notes a stronger restricted model, Mythos Preview, at 77.8% deemed too dangerous for public use.
items.source: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones

items.title: Neuroscientist Builds AI That Refuses Answers, Outperforms All Models
items.summary: A neuroscientist developed an AI that only asks questions and refuses direct answers, beating every existing model including those trained on billions of users' data. The approach highlights risks of dependency on answer-providing AIs, akin to GPS effects on memory.

items.title: Google's Gemini 3 Family Advances with 77.1% ARC-AGI-2 Score and Low-Cost Options
items.summary: Gemini 3 lineup includes Flash-Lite at $0.25 per million tokens, real-time voice with Flash Live, scoring 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2 (from under 5% prior). Compares favorably to GPT-4o, Llama 3, and Claude 3 in benchmarks.

items.title: $1 AI Guardrails Counter Rising LLM Attacks in Production Systems
items.summary: Talk details six common attack vectors like prompt injection, RAG poisoning, and agentic escalation, now baseline threats. Highlights websites embedding manipulative prompts to bias AI decisions, with documented overruling of AI-based processes.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Battlefield 6 Reveals 2026 Roadmap with New Maps, Naval Warfare, and Proximity Chat
items.summary: Battlefield 6 announced a detailed 2026 roadmap including Season 3, 4, and 5 maps, naval warfare features, and proximity chat. The update covers developments through the end of the year.

items.title: Major Nintendo Switch 2 Leaks Including AAA Games like Starfield and Diablo
items.summary: New leaks reveal AAA titles such as Starfield, Diablo, and others rated for Nintendo Switch 2, alongside rumors of raids on Splatoon leakers and potential remakes. Nintendo's Spotlight Sale highlights third-party deals.

items.title: Helldivers 2 Developers Fix Super Earth Error and Address Nerfs
items.summary: Arrowhead Game Studios fixed a Super Earth error, updated known issues, and players discovered nerfs to FRV and Sentry Stratagem. Sony filed a new controller patent.

items.title: Fall Guys Receives Limited Fortnite Crossover Mode After Hiatus
items.summary: Fall Guys developer Mediatonic announced a single limited-time 3v3 map mode for Fortnite following a multi-month hiatus. The update comes amid past Epic Games layoffs affecting the studio.
items.source: YouTube (Death of a Game: Fall Guys)

items.title: 2026 Video Game News Roundup: Switch 2 Rumors, Pokemon Issues, and Studio Closures
items.summary: Tim discusses key 2026 news including Nintendo Switch 2 boost mode, Pokemon controversies, leaks on Ocarina of Time remake, Super Metroid rumors, and Blue Point Studio shutting down.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Silicon Quantum Computer
items.summary: Quantum Motion has achieved a milestone by deploying the first full-stack quantum computer using standard silicon chip fabrication processes, potentially accelerating scalable quantum systems.[1]

items.title: Google and Qualcomm Push Android PCs into Mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are advancing Android PCs to challenge Microsoft and Apple, aiming to scale the mobile OS to desktops and reshape personal computing.[1]

items.title: Amazon Faces Class-Action Lawsuit Over 'Bricking' Fire TV Sticks
items.summary: A class-action suit accuses Amazon of intentionally disabling early Fire TV streaming sticks to force upgrades, drawing significant consumer backlash.[2]

items.title: iPad Air Reportedly Switching to OLED Displays Next Year
items.summary: Sources claim the iPad Air will upgrade to OLED screens, potentially marking one of its biggest enhancements, though another iPad model might launch it first.[2]

items.title: YouTube Introduces Option to Disable Shorts Entirely on Mobile
items.summary: YouTube now allows users to turn off Shorts completely on mobile devices, addressing user preferences for a customized viewing experience.[2]

items.title: Samsung Expands Galaxy A55 One UI 8.5 Beta to More Countries
items.summary: The One UI 8.5 beta program for Galaxy A55 is now live in additional countries, bringing features originally for Galaxy S26 to older models like S24.[2]
